title: handle_inbox checks signed header list with Vec::contains (O(n) per check)
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:787-803

FINDING: The code collects the space-separated signed_headers string into a Vec<&str> and then calls .contains() four times in a row. Each contains() is O(n) over the vec. The vec is always short (typically 4 headers), so this is correct and the absolute cost is negligible.

However, a cleaner rewrite would collect into a HashSet or check inline during the initial split — not for performance, but because repeating .contains() four times with different string literals makes the code look like it might have a complexity concern when it doesn't. The improvement is to convert to a single HashSet at construction and call .contains() naturally, making the intent (set membership) match the data structure.
close_reason: Accepted: Vec::contains on 4 items is effectively O(1). HashSet would be slower.
